ab: Nanu wawa, na i Rinahem ku luma’ niyam, micudad satu, tayraen i Tawyen a malinah, i tokatokayan satu a maurip.
ch: 小時候我們家住在光榮部落，上學以後，就搬到了桃園市，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Rinahem. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Micudad satu kaku i Tasiye, u serangawan nu Yincumin, ku nitutucan nu maku a minanam, u sakavana’ tu nikasarumaruma a serangawan nu i Taywanay a yaanay ku aru’ a vinacadan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Sisa, a taluma’ kaku atu kaka tu vavainayan i pipantangan nina mihca mangalay a milisu’ tu kalas nu niyaru’, mangalay a misapinang tu serangawan nu urip.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Walu a pulu’ tu ku mihcaan nu matuasay, nika matiya i terungay hen ku mihcaan a melawen.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Pina saw ku nikavana’an nu misu tu suwal nu rumaan a niyaru’? mavana’ cira a semuwal tunu Ripun, nu Teruku atu nu Ngayngay.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的日語說得很流利，也會說太魯閣族語和客家語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Teruku, as well as Atayal, Hakka and Ngayngay.

ab: Palita han nu maku cira, araw nanu wawa mavana’tu tu suwal nu Ripun, siramud sa u vavahi nu Teruku, i nikadademak aluman ku cavay nira tu Ngayngay, namahaen hati:la satu ku kavana’an nira tu rumaan a suwal.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是太魯閣族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Teruku, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: I nipilisu’ niyam a masasuwal, adihay ku nipasevana’ ni vaki tu kakawaw tunu sakay pivutingan, piadupan a rayrayan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於漁獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Kanahatu caay katesek ku nikavana’an niyam tu nipisaheci ni vaki, nika, luingen niyam tu soci, hangay liyawan nu niyam a misapinang a mitengil i luma’.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Yu wawa hen kami nahacuwa a mavana’ tu serangawan nu tireng, mangudu aca a kavana’an nu tau tu wawa nu Pangcah.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Adihay ku nikavana’an tu serangawan nu tuas, mangalev ku nikariang a malawawa nu Pangcah, u masacidekay, u kasenengan ku vinacadan nu mita.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為阿美族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a ’Amis is unique and something to be proud of.
